 {"id":"bd-cz3rv","title":"fuzz: cover parse_dx_root_with_large_dir large_dir=true","description":"ffs_ondisk parse_dx_root_with_large_dir large_dir=true path not exercised","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:49:10.394062450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:43:57Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T00:43:57Z","close_reason":"Extended fuzz_ext4_htree_mmp to exercise parse_dx_root_with_large_dir(true) alongside the normal path and verified with remote cargo check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-d4wv","title":"Fix clippy pedantic lint in ffs-mvcc wal_writer.rs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:49:21.511715985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T03:49:21.670130006Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T03:49:21.669908577Z","close_reason":"Fixed: converted match to let...else per clippy pedantic lint","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-d5ll","title":"Add block_size validation before u32-to-usize cast in ffs-repair pipeline","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-20T00:48:51.207643591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T00:53:25.148733218Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T00:53:25.148715475Z","close_reason":"All block_size casts in ffs-repair/pipeline.rs are in #[cfg(test)] code only — no production risk","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-dc9y5","title":"[FUSE] mknod for char/block devices (overlayfs whiteout prerequisite)","description":"Follow-up to bd-z01zm: dispatch_mknod in ffs-fuse currently rejects every non-regular file type with EOPNOTSUPP. Required for overlayfs-as-upper-layer parity (whiteouts use mknod with S_IFCHR + rdev=makedev(0,0)) and for general POSIX mknod compatibility (FIFOs, sockets, char/block devices).

Required:
- Add FsOps::mknod(parent, name, mode, rdev, uid, gid) -> InodeAttr trait method with default impl returning ENOTSUP.
- OpenFs::ext4_mknod: route through ffs_inode::create_inode with the full mode (no S_IFREG OR), then for char/block devices clear EXT4_EXTENTS_FL and encode rdev into extent_bytes[0..4] (old format) or [4..8] (new format) per ext4_inode.h. For FIFO/socket use mode bits only (no rdev needed).
- Update dispatch_mknod to forward S_IFCHR/S_IFBLK/S_IFIFO/S_IFSOCK to ops.mknod() instead of rejecting.
- Tests: mknod whiteout char device (rdev=0), readback via getattr returns S_IFCHR + rdev=0; mknod block device with rdev=makedev(8,1), readback returns rdev encoded matching device_number().
- Then exercise overlayfs via xfstests overlay/{006,037,069}.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:18:30.774015760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:18:30.774015760Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-dc9y5","title":"[FUSE] mknod for char/block devices (overlayfs whiteout prerequisite)","description":"Follow-up to bd-z01zm: dispatch_mknod in ffs-fuse currently rejects every non-regular file type with EOPNOTSUPP. Required for overlayfs-as-upper-layer parity (whiteouts use mknod with S_IFCHR + rdev=makedev(0,0)) and for general POSIX mknod compatibility (FIFOs, sockets, char/block devices).

Required:
- Add FsOps::mknod(parent, name, mode, rdev, uid, gid) -> InodeAttr trait method with default impl returning ENOTSUP.
- OpenFs::ext4_mknod: route through ffs_inode::create_inode with the full mode (no S_IFREG OR), then for char/block devices clear EXT4_EXTENTS_FL and encode rdev into extent_bytes[0..4] (old format) or [4..8] (new format) per ext4_inode.h. For FIFO/socket use mode bits only (no rdev needed).
- Update dispatch_mknod to forward S_IFCHR/S_IFBLK/S_IFIFO/S_IFSOCK to ops.mknod() instead of rejecting.
- Tests: mknod whiteout char device (rdev=0), readback via getattr returns S_IFCHR + rdev=0; mknod block device with rdev=makedev(8,1), readback returns rdev encoded matching device_number().
- Then exercise overlayfs via xfstests overlay/{006,037,069}.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:18:30.774015760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T11:27:36.352994976Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T11:27:36.351076023Z","close_reason":"FsOps::mknod trait method + OpenFs::ext4_mknod + dispatch_mknod fan-out shipped. char/block devices encode rdev in i_block (old 16-bit format preferred when rdev fits, new 32-bit format otherwise); FIFO/socket reject non-zero rdev with EINVAL; S_IFREG via mknod is rejected (wrong-API misuse - use create). 5 new ffs-core regressions cover whiteout round-trip, /dev/sda1-style rdev preservation, FIFO+socket creation, FIFO+rdev EINVAL, S_IFREG-via-mknod EINVAL. btrfs returns ENOTSUP until a separate btrfs mknod path lands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-devbv","title":"add mounted-path E2E and ioctl-trace coverage for ext4 EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION","description":"Survey finding: `FsOps::get_inode_version` exists in `crates/ffs-core/src/vfs.rs`, `ffs-fuse` dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION`, and `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` has direct dispatch tests, but `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` has no mounted-path proof for the ioctl.

Scope:
- Add a mounted-path helper in `fuse_e2e.rs` that issues `EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION` against a real ext4 file over FUSE.
- Assert the ioctl-trace log records the command when the kernel forwards it, and soft-skip only when the kernel/VFS rejects before userspace dispatch.
- Verify stable value encoding and buffer-size/errno behavior against the mounted path, not only the unit-dispatch shim.
- Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` if operator-visible coverage now exists.

Acceptance:
- At least one mounted-path `EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION` scenario exists with deterministic assertions.
- CI artifacts make it clear whether failures are transport-layer skips or FrankenFS regressions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:16:09.486713081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:04:21.391364637Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:04:21.389261856Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fuse","ioctl","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-dhk8","title":"Add ext4 inline-data EOF read-boundary conformance","description":"No ready beads remain. Add conformance coverage for ext4 INLINE_DATA read boundaries using existing fixtures: reads ending at EOF, reads past EOF, and continuation-tail bounds should return exact bytes/empty output without over-read. Update coverage docs and validate via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:24:50.830366504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:30:40.624041635Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T02:30:40.621632836Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 inline-data EOF/read-boundary conformance coverage and updated coverage totals; focused test plus workspace check/clippy passed via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","inline-data"]}
 {"id":"bd-dj2pm","title":"add mounted-path btrfs symlink occupied-destination EEXIST and non-directory-parent ENOTDIR coverage","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path FUSE E2E proves symlink creation and readlink success, but the public refusal contract for an occupied destination name or non-directory parent is not frozen even though ffs-core already returns Exists and NotADirectory. Scope: add btrfs mounted-path probes that assert exact EEXIST for existing names and ENOTDIR for non-directory parents, with unchanged directory contents and no partial mutation. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes btrfs symlink refusal behavior for these operator-visible cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:18.497089634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:09:58.000960289Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:09:57.998183582Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
